messages.content:
You are a research assistant with access to web search and URL fetching tools.

Work step by step:
1. Search for relevant information
2. Fetch and read important pages in detail
3. Search again if you need more specific facts
4. Synthesize into a clear, sourced answer

When you have a complete answer, respond in plain text without calling any tool.

I’m researching species that became invasive after people who kept them as pets released them. There’s a certain species of fish that was popularized as a pet by being the main character of the movie Finding Nemo. According to the USGS, where was this fish found as a nonnative species, before the year 2020? I need the answer formatted as the five-digit zip codes of the places the species was found, separated by commas if there is more than one place.

message.content:
<tool_call>
{"name": "web_search", "arguments": {"query": "Finding Nemo fish invasive species USGS zip codes"}}
</tool_call>

function.name: web_search
function.arguments: {"query": "Finding Nemo fish invasive species USGS zip codes"}

args.query: Finding Nemo fish invasive species USGS zip codes

response.content:
Title: Nonindigenous Aquatic Species
URL: https://nas.er.usgs.gov/SEINED/
Snippet: SEINeD - Screen and Evaluate Invasive and Non-native Data Identifying the leading edge of an invasion can be difficult, especially when the invader is a native transplant, or if the invader is not well-known. This tool allows a user to upload a biological dataset collected anywhere in the conterminous U.S., Alaska, Hawaii, or Island Territories to screen for occurrences of non-native and ...
---
Title: US Fish and Wildlife Service Open Data
URL: https://gis-fws.opendata.arcgis.com/
Snippet: Discover, analyze and download data from US Fish and Wildlife Service Open Data. Download in CSV, KML, Zip, GeoJSON, GeoTIFF or PNG. Find API links for GeoServices, WMS, and WFS. Analyze with charts and thematic maps. Take the next step and create storymaps and webmaps.
---
Title: Invasive Species Experts Database | U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service
URL: https://www.fws.gov/program/aquatic-nuisance-species-task-force/invasive-species-experts-database
Snippet: ANS Task Force Experts Directory The Invasive Species Experts Database is a resource intended to help find the right expert to address specific invasive species management question or identify a species. In 2024, the Invasive Species Experts Database (ISED) was incorporated into the U.S. Geological Survey's Siren program and expanded to include both aquatic and terrestrial species experts ...
---
Title: USGS Nonindigenous Aquatic Species database
URL: https://www.gbif.org/dataset/d6cc311c-c5ab-4f23-9a20-10514f9eb9c4
Snippet: This database has been established as a central repository for spatially referenced biogeographic accounts of introduced aquatic species. The program provides scientific reports, online/realtime queries, spatial data sets, regional contact lists, and general information. The data is made available for use by biologists, interagency groups, and the general public.
---
Title: Databases | National Invasive Species Information Center
URL: https://www.invasivespeciesinfo.gov/type/databases
Snippet: USGS published a dataset called "Catalog of U.S. Federal Early Detection/Rapid Response Invasive Species Databases and Tools." The catalog, developed in collaboration with the National Invasive Species Council Secretariat, is a multi-sheet spreadsheet that contains openly available, online, federally supported databases and tools dealing with various aspects of a potential national early ...

message.content:
Based on the information provided, the USGS Nonindigenous Aquatic Species database is a central repository for spatially referenced biogeographic accounts of introduced aquatic species. However, the specific zip codes where the Finding Nemo fish, likely referring to the clownfish (Amphiprioninae), was found as a nonnative species before 2020 are not directly mentioned in the search results. 

The database does not provide the zip codes directly, but it is a good starting point for further research. We should look into the specific zip codes where the clownfish was found as a nonnative species. 

I will now perform a web search to find the specific zip codes where the clownfish was found as a nonnative species before 2020.
<tool_call>
{"name": "web_search", "arguments": {"query": "clownfish Finding Nemo nonnative species zip codes"}}
</tool_call>

function.name: web_search
function.arguments: {"query": "clownfish Finding Nemo nonnative species zip codes"}

args.query: clownfish Finding Nemo nonnative species zip codes

response.content:
Title: Nonindigenous Aquatic Species Database | U.S. Geological Survey
URL: https://www.usgs.gov/centers/wetland-and-aquatic-research-center/science/science-topics/nonindigenous-aquatic-species
Snippet: The USGS Nonindigenous Aquatic Species (NAS) database tracks occurrence data on non-native aquatic plant and animal species throughout the United States, and provides the public with species profiles, distribution maps, and online/real-time queries for state/hydrologic basin-specific species.
---
Title: 24 Most Popular Types of Fish from Finding Nemo - AquAnswers
URL: https://aquanswers.com/fish-from-finding-nemo/
Snippet: In this post, I will list the real species of fish from the film Finding Nemo. The famous Nemo, for example, is an actual type of clownfish, and his behavior in the movie is based on real-life traits. After I figured that out, I went ahead and researched what kind of fishes are Dory, Gill, Deb, and the rest of the main characters.
---
Title: Finding Nemo real-life species: Meet the actual sea creatures that ...
URL: https://www.discoverwildlife.com/animal-facts/marine-animals/finding-nemo-species
Snippet: Finding Nemo real-life species: Meet the actual sea creatures that inspired the Pixar classic Think you know your clownfish from your cleaner shrimp? We dive into the real-life species behind the beloved characters of Finding Nemo - and reveal just how niche (and accurate) Pixar got with their casting
---
Title: Dataset - Catalog
URL: https://catalog.data.gov/dataset/?q=zip+codes+of+non-native+fish+species+from+Finding+Nemo&bureauCode=010:12
Snippet: XML XML 3 datasets found for "zip codes of non-native fish species from Finding Nemo"
---
Title: All the fishes featured in Finding Nemo - The Aquarium Guide
URL: https://theaquariumguide.com/articles/fishes-featured-in-finding-nemo
Snippet: Explore the fish species featured in Finding Nemo and learn about their appearance, characteristics, and role in the movie.
